title: Restart macOS gxserver when bundled server build identity changes
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. GxserverClient.startOrReuse accepts any authenticated same-protocol daemon with required tools, so an app update can keep talking to an old running gxserver and miss server-side fixes. Add a gxserver package/build fingerprint to health/runtime metadata and compare it against the bundled expected value, or otherwise force restart on same-protocol package mismatch.

title: Avoid shell argv limits for zmx send payloads
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. sendSessionText/sendSessionMessage accept body payloads up to the JSON limit but embed text into a /bin/zsh -lc script argument. Payloads below the HTTP cap can still exceed OS argv limits or fail at spawn time. Add explicit text-size limits and/or pass payloads through stdin/temp file/direct zmx invocation.

title: Make legacy migration crash-idempotent before completion marker
description: High migration deploy risk. If gxserver exits after inserting DB rows but before recording migration metadata, next launch treats import as notRun and imports duplicate P/G projects/sessions. Add idempotent lookup/upsert by legacyProjectId/legacySessionId or record resumable progress safely.

title: Classify zmx list/probe failures as unknown, not missing
description: High server deploy risk from SERVER_REVIEW_NOTES.md. probeZmxSession uses a shell pipeline where zmx list failure with no output is indistinguishable from grep miss, causing missing classification and bad attach/startup decisions.

title: Use Swift-equivalent freshness when gxserver reads legacy shared vs WK storage
description: Medium migration risk. gxserver readLegacyStateSnapshot always prefers shared JSON when present, while Swift scores WK localStorage vs shared file. Direct gxserver start or startup ordering can migrate stale shared files and mark import complete.

title: Implement or remove gx CLI session interaction endpoints
description: High server deploy risk. API catalog and gx CLI route focus/read/send session commands to gxserver endpoints that dispatch as notImplemented. Either implement focus/read/send APIs or stop advertising/routing them before cutover.

title: Make legacy log import crash-idempotent
description: Second-round Low finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Legacy logs are appended to gxserver.jsonl before the SQLite migration marker is recorded, so a crash after log append but before marker can append duplicate migrated log lines on next launch. This does not corrupt project/session state, but can make upgrade diagnostics noisy. Add idempotency keys/checkpoints or explicitly document and test acceptable duplicate behavior.

title: Make WK localStorage database selection deterministic and freshness-aware
description: Medium migration risk. findLocalStorageDatabases reads every localstorage.sqlite3 and readLegacyLocalStorageValues takes first value by enumeration order. Multiple WebKit DBs can import stale settings/agents/project commands.

title: Add timeout and output caps to typed subprocess operations
description: High server deploy risk. runGitAction/runWorktreeAction/runBeadsAction buffer stdout/stderr without timeout or size cap, allowing long hangs or unbounded memory growth.

title: Keep gxserver local listener fixed or fail invalid config
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. Config merging allows listeners.local.host/port to override the fixed local API listener, while runtime metadata, status checks, CLI, docs, and macOS/sidebar clients assume 127.0.0.1:58744. Make local host/port immutable or fail startup with a clear validation error if configured.

title: Restrict CORS and private-network headers to trusted gxserver clients
description: Medium server risk. applyCorsHeaders returns Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Access-Control-Allow-Private-Network: true for all listeners. Auth protects APIs, but browser attack surface is broader than needed.

title: Surface corrupted SQLite JSON columns instead of silently emptying state
description: Low server risk. domain-state JSON parse helpers return {} or [] on corruption, which can hide persistence corruption and allow later updates to overwrite data with empty state.

title: Define and enforce settings freshness source policy during migration
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Settings import currently prefers any native-sidebar-settings.json over WK ghostex-native-settings with no freshness/richness scoring. Decide the intended source of truth and implement deterministic behavior so stale shared settings cannot silently override newer WK settings unless that is explicitly correct.

title: Do not mark zmx provider missing when kill fails
description: High server deploy risk. sleepSession/killSession persists sleeping/stopped plus provider missing even when zmx kill failed. This can lose route to live sessions.

title: Make SSH gxserver profiles establish and manage tunnels before RPC
description: High remote deploy risk. CLI resolves SSH forwardPlan but requestGxserverRpc fetches baseUrl directly; default local forward port can conflict with local gxserver. Implement tunnel check/start or clear user-facing failure guidance.

title: Avoid exposing credential tokens through process argv
description: Low server risk. credential-store writes use security add-generic-password -w <secret>, and CLI supports one-shot --token. Local process listing/history can expose bearer tokens.

title: Clean up local listener if remote listener startup fails
description: Medium server risk. runGxserverForeground starts local listener then remote listener; if remote bind fails, local listener may remain half-started without runtime metadata.

title: Bound domain-state JSON payload size and depth
description: Medium server risk. Project/session APIs accept unbounded nested JSON into SQLite, making durable oversized rows and huge list responses possible.

title: Canonicalize previous-session history stored in gxserver DB
description: Medium migration risk. previousSessionHistoryJson is stored from legacy previous sessions without recursive ID remap, unlike shared previous-sessions JSON. gxserver clients may receive legacy project/session IDs.

title: Bound gxserver readSessionText zmx history output
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. /api/readSessionText returns full zmx history output and runZshScript accumulates stdout/stderr without caps, so authenticated clients can force large memory/JSON responses from long-running sessions. Add byte/line limits, truncation metadata, pagination, streaming, or equivalent bounded behavior before stdout accumulation becomes unbounded.

title: Bound Beads board reads in gxserver typed operations
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_SERVER_REVIEW.md. runBeadsAction board bypasses bounded subprocess handling and reads/parses the entire .beads/issues.jsonl file into memory. Add file-size, row-count, and/or response-size bounds with truncation metadata or explicit typed errors.

title: Remap project-board bead conversation links in gxserver DB import
description: High migration deploy risk. shared sidebar rewrite remaps ghostexSessionId/sessionId/projectId, but projectBoardConfigJson imports beadConversationLinks directly with legacy g-* IDs. Remap links before storing projectBoardConfig in SQLite.

title: Block sidebar hydration until gxserver migration is canonical
description: Critical migration deploy risk from MIGRATION_REVIEW_NOTES.md. On first upgraded launch, AppDelegate can create the window/sidebar before gxserver migration rewrites shared state. React can hydrate stale project-*/g-* IDs and later write them back over canonical P/G files. Ensure migration completes before sidebar hydration or force a canonical state reload before any sidebar persistence.

title: Make log querying bounded for large JSONL files
description: Medium server risk. queryLogs reads, filters, sorts the whole gxserver.jsonl before applying limit. Long-running installs can spike memory/CPU.

title: Enforce HTTP request body size limits
description: Medium server risk. readRequestJson buffers authenticated POST bodies without a size limit. Remote authenticated clients can force memory growth.

title: Remap or exempt legacy browser and T3 pane IDs during gxserver migration
description: Second-round High finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Migrated shared project snapshots can keep legacy g-* IDs for non-terminal browser/T3 panes. Because the rewritten shared file is marked gxserverMigratedAt, React treats it as canonical and can block future persistence when outgoing payloads still contain those IDs. Decide and implement the correct policy: either allocate/remap every session-bearing pane to canonical G IDs, or narrow legacy-ID detection/remapping to daemon-owned terminal IDs only if browser/T3 IDs intentionally remain client-local.

title: Canonicalize empty previous-sessions shared state during migration
description: Low migration risk. rewriteSharedSidebarStateWithGxserverIds skips writing previous-sessions file when snapshot is empty, and Swift does not consider empty arrays migrated. Old WK previous-session history may reappear.

title: Include command-panel sessions in migration freshness scoring
description: Second-round Medium finding from SECOND_ROUND_MIGRATION_REVIEW.md. Shared-vs-WK projectSnapshotScore only counts workspace grid sessions, while importer also migrates commandsPanel.sessions. A richer WK payload can lose to stale shared JSON if the only newer state is command-panel sessions or active command references.

title: Auto-Symlink Env Files
description:
I want to add a feature that auto-symlinks .env files from the main directory whenever I make a worktree

name = "Worktree: MY REPO"

[[panes]]
commands = [
    "git worktree add -b {{autogenerated_branch_name}} ~/.warp/worktrees/MY_REPO/{{autogenerated_branch_name}}",
    "cd ~/.warp/worktrees/MY_REPO/{{autogenerated_branch_name}}",
    "find ~/Projects/MY_REPO -type f -name '.env.*' -exec sh -c 'for f do rel=\"${f#~/Projects/MY_REPO/}\"; [ -e \"$rel\" ] || [ -L \"$rel\" ] && continue; mkdir -p \"$(dirname \"$rel\")\"; ln -s \"$f\" \"$rel\"; done' sh {} +",
    "pnpm i"
]
directory = "~/Projects/MY_REPO"
id = "main"
type = "terminal"

title: Session focus mode UX fixes
description:
Implemented session focus mode UX changes from this conversation:

- Double-click/context-menu Focus on sidebar cards and native pane tabs enters reversible focus mode for split panes.
- Focus mode switches to Agents and remembers/restores the previous project view on exit.
- Split focus expands the focused pane while preserving related top tabs for that pane group.
- Clicking same-group tabs works while focused.
- Clicking tabs outside the focused group exits focus and focuses the target tab group.
- Added Exit focus titlebar control, matched it to titlebar tab sizing, and fixed native hit-region routing.
- Prevented focus mode for single-pane tab groups; single-pane double-click now behaves as normal tab selection.
- A second Focus request for the already focused split pane exits focus mode.

Verified with shared workspace-state tests and typecheck.

title: Generating title...
description:
after finishing working on a bead we need to tell agents to write in the comments what was done (after each turn)

with a focus on the user facing requirements and high level techincal details (no need for specific lines changed)

comments.author: madda
comments.text: Start Work now tells agents to run `bd comment <id> "<summary>"` after each turn with progress. Comments should describe user-facing outcomes and high-level technical approach, not file/line diffs. Same guidance added to AGENTS.md project board workflow. Covered by buildAgentWorkPrompt unit test.
